mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month growth in industrial production (INDPRO) reflects real-time manufacturing and utility output expansion, which translates into higher corporate sales and earnings, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Positive month-over-month INDPRO growth coincides with negative SPX 20-day forward returns in more than 55% of instances over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A rise in the unemployment rate of more than 0.3 percentage points over a three-month window signals deteriorating labor income and household demand expectations, causing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: When the trigger fires, SPX 60-day forward returns are positive in more than 50% of triggered instances or the mean return is not significantly negative (t-test p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous month-over-month declines in retail sales (RSAFS) and housing starts (HOUST) indicate synchronized weakness in consumer spending and residential investment, amplifying aggregate demand shortfalls and generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint trigger occurs but SPX 20-day forward returns are positive in more than 50% of instances, or either proxy used alone yields higher directional accuracy than the pair.

mechanism_narrative: During monetary tightening cycles, an elevated federal funds rate (FEDFUNDS) above 3.5% raises the equity discount rate and debt service costs, compressing valuation multiples and causing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days; this mechanism is muted outside tightening regimes.
failure_condition: During identified tightening cycles with FEDFUNDS > 3.5%, SPX 5-day forward returns are positive in more than 50% of instances or the Rank IC between FEDFUNDS and forward returns is greater than -0.03.

mechanism_narrative: Quarter-over-quarter growth in real GDP (GDPC1) captures the economy's aggregate income and spending trajectory, leading positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days as equity valuations align with macro expansion.
failure_condition: Positive GDPC1 growth quarters coincide with negative SPX 60-day forward returns in more than 55% of instances over any 5-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A decline in industrial production (INDPRO) exceeding 1.0% over a three-month period signals capacity underutilization and weakening corporate pricing power, driving negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: When the trigger fires, SPX 20-day forward returns are positive in more than 50% of triggered instances or average returns are not significantly negative (t-test p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month payroll employment (PAYEMS) growth signals labor market momentum that drives consumption and earnings, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Positive PAYEMS momentum coincides with negative SPX 5-day forward returns in more than 55% of instances over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A year-over-year increase in consumer price inflation (CPIAUCSL) above 3.0% signals macro overheating and induces forward-looking monetary tightening expectations, raising real discount rates and generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: When CPIAUCSL year-over-year change exceeds 3.0%, SPX 60-day forward returns are positive in more than 50% of triggered instances.
